diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Arjuna (Terminalia arjuna): Premier Hridya (cardiotonic) in Ayurveda. Published RCTs show improvement in LVEF, exercise tolerance, and symptom reduction in chronic heart failure. Bark powder 500mg-1g twice daily with milk. Arjunarishta 15-30ml twice daily. Most evidence-based Ayurvedic cardiac herb.

Pushkaramoola (Inula racemosa): Classical Hridya herb for cardiac conditions. Anti-anginal and cardioprotective properties. Often combined with Arjuna. 1-3g powder twice daily.

Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera): Adaptogenic cardioprotective. Reduces stress-related cardiac damage. 300-600mg extract daily. May support cardiac function and exercise tolerance.

Pippali (Piper longum): Used in heart failure for its Hridya and bioavailability-enhancing properties. Part of Trikatu. Improves circulation.

Arjunarishta: Classical fermented preparation of Arjuna bark. Most widely used Ayurvedic heart tonic in India. 15-30ml with equal water twice daily after meals.

Hridayarnava Ras: Classical herbo-mineral for cardiac conditions. Contains Suvarna Bhasma, Mauktik Bhasma. Strict practitioner supervision.

Dabur Arjunarishta: Widely available branded formulation. Affordable cardiac tonic.

Himalaya Arjuna Capsules: Standardized Arjuna bark extract. 1-2 capsules twice daily.
diseases.india_specific:
Rising Epidemic in India: Rheumatic heart disease (from untreated streptococcal pharyngitis in childhood) remains a major cause of heart failure in young Indians — unlike the West where coronary disease dominates. Estimated 25-30 million Indians have heart failure.

Hypertension-Diabetes-CAD Triad: Uncontrolled hypertension and diabetes accelerate coronary artery disease and heart failure. India's metabolic syndrome epidemic directly feeds the heart failure burden.

Rheumatic Heart Disease: India has the highest global RHD burden. Mitral stenosis/regurgitation from childhood rheumatic fever causes heart failure in 20-40 year olds. Preventable with Penicillin treatment of streptococcal throat infections.

Late Presentation: Most Indian patients present with advanced (NYHA III-IV) heart failure. Limited access to echocardiography, cardiologists, and cardiac surgery in rural India delays diagnosis and treatment.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — Medications Are Lifelong: Heart failure requires lifelong medication — ACEi, Beta-blocker, MRA, and diuretics. Stopping any of these causes rapid deterioration and hospitalization. Patients must understand: these medicines keep the heart functioning, not cure it.

Arjuna Has Genuine Evidence: Terminalia arjuna is one of the few Ayurvedic herbs with published RCT evidence in heart failure. It can complement standard therapy but CANNOT replace ACEi/Beta-blockers. Use alongside, not instead of, prescribed cardiac medications.

Fluid & Salt Restriction: Restrict fluid to 1.5-2 liters/day and salt to <2g/day. Daily weight monitoring — gain >2 kg in 3 days = fluid overload, increase diuretic. This simple self-monitoring prevents emergency hospitalization.

Avoid NSAIDs & Certain Drugs: Ibuprofen, Diclofenac, and Naproxen worsen heart failure by causing fluid retention. Verapamil and Diltiazem are contraindicated in HFrEF. Metformin needs caution if eGFR <30. Always check drug interactions.

Rheumatic Heart Disease — Preventable: RHD is a major cause of heart failure in young Indians. Prevention: treat streptococcal sore throat with Penicillin. Secondary prophylaxis: monthly Benzathine Penicillin injections for diagnosed RHD. This prevents valvular damage and heart failure.

diseases.name: Hepatitis
diseases.tests:
1. Liver Function Tests (LFT)

Serum Bilirubin (Total & Direct): Elevated total bilirubin (>1.2 mg/dL) confirms jaundice. Direct (conjugated) bilirubin elevated in hepatocellular and obstructive jaundice.

AST/ALT (Transaminases): Markedly elevated (>10x normal, often >1000 IU/L) in acute viral hepatitis. ALT typically > AST. Most sensitive markers of hepatocyte damage.

Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP) & GGT: Elevated in cholestatic hepatitis and biliary obstruction.

Serum Albumin & PT/INR: Low albumin and prolonged PT/INR indicate impaired hepatic synthetic function — markers of severity.

2. Viral Markers (Specific Diagnosis)

HBsAg: Surface antigen — positive in acute and chronic Hep B. First marker to appear. Persistence >6 months = chronic HBV.

Anti-HBc IgM: Positive in acute Hep B. Differentiates acute from chronic.

Anti-HCV Antibody + HCV RNA (PCR): Anti-HCV positive = exposure. HCV RNA PCR confirms active infection and guides DAA treatment.

IgM Anti-HAV: Positive = acute Hep A infection.

IgM Anti-HEV: Positive = acute Hep E infection. Critical in pregnant women.

HBV DNA (Viral Load): Quantifies HBV replication. Guides antiviral treatment decisions. >2000 IU/mL with elevated ALT = treatment indication.

3. Imaging

Ultrasound Abdomen: Hepatomegaly, altered echotexture, portal hypertension signs (splenomegaly, ascites), gallbladder wall edema.

FibroScan (Transient Elastography): Non-invasive liver stiffness measurement. Assesses fibrosis stage without biopsy. Increasingly available in Indian cities.

4. Key Diagnostic Criteria

Acute Hepatitis: Jaundice + ALT >10x normal + positive viral markers (IgM class). Chronic Hepatitis B: HBsAg positive >6 months. Chronic Hepatitis C: Anti-HCV + HCV RNA positive. Acute Liver Failure: Jaundice + coagulopathy (INR >1.5) + encephalopathy within 26 weeks of illness onset.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. Hepatitis A & E (Supportive — No Specific Antiviral)

Supportive Care: Rest, adequate nutrition, hydration. Avoid hepatotoxic drugs (Paracetamol in high doses, alcohol). Most cases resolve spontaneously in 4-8 weeks. Hospitalize if: INR >1.5, persistent vomiting, encephalopathy.

2. Hepatitis B (Chronic)

Tenofovir Disoproxil Fumarate (TDF): 300mg once daily. First-line for chronic HBV. Potent, high barrier to resistance. Long-term/lifelong treatment. Available as affordable generic in India.

Entecavir: 0.5-1mg once daily. Alternative first-line. High efficacy, low resistance.

Pegylated Interferon-α: 180μg SC weekly for 48 weeks. Finite treatment duration but many side effects. Selected patients only.

3. Hepatitis C (CURABLE)

Sofosbuvir + Velpatasvir (Pan-Genotypic): 400/100mg once daily for 12 weeks. Cures >95% of all HCV genotypes. India manufactures generic versions at ₹5,000-10,000 per course (vs $84,000 globally). Game-changing for India.

Sofosbuvir + Daclatasvir: Alternative DAA combination. 12-24 weeks based on genotype and cirrhosis status.

4. Acute Liver Failure

IV Fluids, Glucose, FFP for coagulopathy, Lactulose/Rifaximin for encephalopathy. Liver transplant evaluation if INR >3 or Grade 3-4 encephalopathy.

5. Prevention

Hepatitis B Vaccine: Part of India's UIP. 3 doses (birth, 6 weeks, 14 weeks as Pentavalent). 95% effective. Prevent mother-to-child: HBsAg screening in pregnancy + HBIG + vaccine for neonates of positive mothers.

Hepatitis A Vaccine: Available privately. 2 doses. Recommended by IAP.

Safe Water + Hygiene: Prevents Hep A & E. Boil water during outbreaks.

Safe Blood & Injection Practices: Screen all blood donations. Use disposable needles/syringes. Prevent Hep B & C.
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Paracetamol Tablets IP 500mg: Use cautiously — limited to 2g/day max in liver disease. Pack of 10.

ORS Powder: For hydration support during acute hepatitis.

Tenofovir Tablets IP 300mg: First-line for chronic HBV. Pack of 30.

Sofosbuvir + Velpatasvir Tablets (400/100mg): Pan-genotypic HCV cure. Pack of 28.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):

Tenofovir 300mg, 30's: ₹120.00

ORS sachet: ₹3.50
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Bhumyamalaki / Phyllanthus niruri (Bhumi Amla): Most studied Ayurvedic hepatoprotective. Published evidence for anti-HBV activity (reduces HBsAg). Classical Yakrit Shodhaka (liver cleanser). 500mg-1g twice daily.

Katuki / Kutki (Picrorhiza kurroa): Premier hepatoprotective in Ayurveda. Kutkin compound shows hepatoprotective activity. For Kamala (jaundice) and Yakrit Vikara (liver disorders). 500mg-1g twice daily.

Kalmegh / Bhunimba (Andrographis paniculata): Potent hepatoprotective and Jwarahara. Andrographolide compound shows anti-inflammatory and liver-protective activity. 500mg-1g twice daily.

Arogyavardhini Vati: Classical formulation specifically for Kamala (jaundice) and liver disorders. Contains Kutki, Triphala, Shilajeet, Loha Bhasma. 2 tablets twice daily.

Punarnava (Boerhaavia diffusa): Classical for Shotha (edema) and liver conditions. Diuretic and hepatoprotective. Punarnava Mandura for associated anemia.

Phalatrikadi Kwatha: Classical decoction for Kamala. Contains Triphala, Guduchi, Daruharidra. 15-30ml twice daily.

Dabur Liv.52 / Himalaya Liv.52: India's most famous proprietary hepatoprotective. Contains Himsra (Capparis spinosa) and Kasani (Cichorium intybus). Published evidence for liver protection. 2 tablets twice daily.

Patanjali Livamrit: Ayurvedic liver tonic with Bhumyamalaki, Kalmegh, Kutki.
diseases.india_specific:
Hepatitis A & E — Waterborne Endemic: India is hyperendemic for Hep A and Hep E. Outbreaks occur during monsoon via contaminated water. Hep E is particularly dangerous in pregnant women (mortality 15-25%). Fecal-oral transmission through contaminated water/food.

Hepatitis B — Chronic Carrier Burden: India has estimated 40 million chronic HBV carriers (3-4% prevalence). Transmitted via blood, sexual contact, and mother-to-child. Leading cause of cirrhosis and hepatocellular carcinoma in India. HBV vaccine is in India's UIP since 2007.

Hepatitis C — Silent Epidemic: Estimated 6-12 million Indians with chronic HCV. Transmitted via blood (unsafe injections, blood transfusions, IV drug use). Now CURABLE with Direct-Acting Antivirals (DAAs). India manufactures generic DAAs at fraction of global cost.

Unsafe Injection Practices: Reuse of needles/syringes by informal healthcare providers, barbers (shaving blades), and IV drug users remains a major Hep B/C transmission route in India.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — Hepatitis B & C Require Medical Treatment: Chronic HBV needs lifelong Tenofovir/Entecavir to prevent cirrhosis and liver cancer. HCV is now CURABLE with 12-week DAA course. Ayurvedic hepatoprotectives (Bhumyamalaki, Kutki, Liv.52) support liver health but CANNOT clear the virus. Do not substitute Ayurveda for antiviral therapy.

Hepatitis E in Pregnancy = Emergency: Hep E in pregnant women (especially third trimester) has 15-25% mortality from acute liver failure. Any pregnant woman with jaundice must be hospitalized IMMEDIATELY for monitoring. No specific treatment — supportive care + early liver transplant referral if deteriorating.

AVOID Paracetamol Overdose: Maximum 2g/day in hepatitis patients (normally 4g/day). Paracetamol overdose causes acute liver failure. Use cautiously. Avoid all alcohol during and for 6 months after hepatitis.

Hepatitis B Vaccine = Cancer Prevention: HBV causes 80% of liver cancer (hepatocellular carcinoma) in India. Vaccinating all children under UIP prevents future HBV infection, cirrhosis, and liver cancer. Screen all pregnant women for HBsAg.

Liv.52 Is Genuinely Supportive: Himalaya Liv.52 is one of the most studied hepatoprotective formulations with published evidence for liver enzyme normalization and liver protection. It can safely complement medical treatment for all types of hepatitis.

diseases.name: Hookworm Infection
diseases.tests:
1. Stool Examination

Stool Microscopy (Ova Detection): Direct/concentration method to detect characteristic hookworm eggs (oval, thin-shelled, 60x40μm, 2-8 cell stage). Single stool sensitivity ~70% — examine 3 samples.

Stool Occult Blood Test: Positive due to intestinal blood loss. Supports diagnosis in endemic settings.

2. Blood Tests

Complete Blood Count: Microcytic hypochromic anemia (low MCV, low MCH), eosinophilia (5-20%). Severity of anemia correlates with worm burden.

Iron Studies: Low serum ferritin, low serum iron, high TIBC. Confirms iron deficiency from chronic blood loss.

Serum Albumin: Low in heavy infections with protein-losing enteropathy.

3. Special Tests

Kato-Katz Technique: Quantitative egg count per gram of stool. Light (<2000 EPG), Moderate (2000-4000), Heavy (>4000). WHO recommended for assessing infection intensity and treatment monitoring.

4. Diagnostic Criteria

Hookworm diagnosed by: Ova in stool microscopy. In endemic areas with anemia + eosinophilia + barefoot soil contact history, empirical treatment with Albendazole is reasonable without waiting for stool confirmation.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. Anti-Helminthic Treatment

Albendazole: 400mg single dose. WHO/NTDD recommended. Cure rate 70-95%. For heavy infections, repeat after 2-4 weeks. Safe, effective, and widely available.

Mebendazole: 500mg single dose or 100mg twice daily for 3 days. Alternative to Albendazole.

Pyrantel Pamoate: 11mg/kg single dose (max 1g). Alternative. Safe in pregnancy (1st trimester — when Albendazole contraindicated).

2. Iron Supplementation (ESSENTIAL alongside deworming)

Ferrous Sulfate: 200mg (65mg elemental iron) 2-3 times daily with Vitamin C for 3-6 months. Deworming alone is insufficient — must replete iron stores.

IV Iron Sucrose: For severe anemia (Hb <7 g/dL) or inability to tolerate oral iron. 200mg IV infusion. Rapid iron repletion.

Blood Transfusion: For life-threatening anemia (Hb <5 g/dL with cardiac compromise).

3. Prevention

Footwear: Wearing shoes/sandals prevents larval skin penetration. Simple, cheap, effective. Promote especially for agricultural workers and children.

National Deworming Day: Biannual mass Albendazole for all children 1-19 years. Encourage 100% participation.

Sanitation: Eliminate open defecation. Proper toilet use breaks the soil transmission cycle.
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Albendazole Tablets IP 400mg: First-line anti-helminthic. Available free on National Deworming Day.

Iron + Folic Acid Tablets: Essential for anemia correction. Available at Jan Aushadhi stores.

Ferrous Ascorbate + Folic Acid: Better tolerated iron formulation.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):

Albendazole 400mg: ₹3.00

Iron + Folic Acid, 10's: ₹10.00
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Vidanga (Embelia ribes): Classical Krimighna (anti-helminthic). Used specifically for intestinal worms in Ayurveda. 3-5g powder with honey on empty stomach.

Palasha Beeja (Butea monosperma seeds): Classical Krimighna. Seeds have demonstrated anti-helminthic activity. 3-5g seed powder on empty stomach for 3 days.

Punarnavadi Mandura: Classical for Pandu (anemia). Contains Punarnava, Mandura Bhasma (iron calx), Triphala. Specifically addresses hookworm-related anemia. 2 tablets twice daily.

Navayasa Churna: Classical iron-rich formulation for Pandu. Contains Loha Bhasma and digestive herbs. 3-5g twice daily with honey.

Lauha Bhasma (Iron Calx): Ayurvedic iron supplement. For Pandu Roga (anemia). 125-250mg twice daily with honey. Practitioner supervision essential.

Dabur Lauhasava: Branded iron-containing Ayurvedic tonic for anemia.

Himalaya Punarnavadi Mandura: Branded classical preparation for anemia.
diseases.india_specific:
Tea Garden & Agricultural Belt: Hookworm thrives in warm, moist soil. Tea plantation workers (Assam, Darjeeling, Nilgiris), barefoot agricultural laborers, and rural populations in tropical India have highest infection rates. Ancylostoma duodenale predominates in North India; Necator americanus in South India.

Open Defecation & Barefoot Walking: The combination of open defecation (contaminating soil with hookworm eggs) and walking barefoot (allowing larval skin penetration) maintains the transmission cycle. Swachh Bharat Mission progress helps break this cycle.

Maternal & Child Health Impact: Hookworm infection in pregnant women causes severe anemia, increasing risk of low birth weight, preterm delivery, and maternal mortality. Deworming in pregnancy reduces anemia.

National Deworming Day: India conducts biannual National Deworming Day (February and August) — mass Albendazole administration to all children aged 1-19 years. World's largest deworming programme.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — Deworm + Iron Together: Deworming (Albendazole) kills worms but does NOT replace lost iron. MUST combine deworming with 3-6 months of iron supplementation for anemia recovery. Treating only one without the other is incomplete treatment.

Wear Shoes — Simplest Prevention: Hookworm larvae penetrate through barefoot skin contact with contaminated soil. Wearing any footwear (even simple chappals) prevents infection. Educate agricultural workers and school children.

National Deworming Day — 100% Participation: Ensure every child aged 1-19 years receives Albendazole during biannual NDD (February and August). It's FREE, safe, and prevents not just hookworm but all soil-transmitted helminths.

Ayurvedic Iron (Lauha/Mandura) Supplements: Punarnavadi Mandura and Navayasa Churna are traditional iron-rich preparations with established use in Pandu (anemia). They can complement modern iron supplementation but take longer to show effect. For severe anemia (Hb <7), use modern iron therapy first.
confirmation_tests_curated: Stool Microscopy (Ova Detection): Direct/concentration method to detect characteristic hookworm eggs (oval, thin-shelled, 60x40μm, 2-8 cell stage). Single stool sensitivity ~70% — examine 3 samples.
Stool Occult Blood Test: Positive due to intestinal blood loss. Supports diagnosis in endemic settings.
Complete Blood Count: Microcytic hypochromic anemia (low MCV, low MCH), eosinophilia (5-20%). Severity of anemia correlates with worm burden.

diseases.name: Japanese Encephalitis
diseases.tests:
1. CSF Analysis (Lumbar Puncture)

CSF Examination: Lymphocytic pleocytosis (50-500 cells/mm³), mildly elevated protein (50-200 mg/dL), normal glucose. Opening pressure may be elevated. Essential to differentiate from bacterial meningitis.

2. JE-Specific Tests

JE IgM ELISA (CSF): Gold standard. JE-specific IgM in CSF confirms diagnosis. Detectable from day 3-4 of illness. Available at sentinel surveillance labs across endemic districts.

JE IgM ELISA (Serum): Supportive — positive serum IgM with clinical encephalitis in endemic area = probable JE. CSF IgM is more specific.

3. Neuroimaging

MRI Brain: Characteristic bilateral thalamic involvement (T2/FLAIR hyperintensity) — highly suggestive of JE. Also shows basal ganglia, midbrain, and cerebral cortex involvement. Helps differentiate from other causes of AES.

CT Brain: Less sensitive than MRI. May show thalamic hypodensity. Useful to exclude mass lesions and hydrocephalus.

4. Other Tests

Complete Blood Count: Mild leukocytosis. Thrombocytopenia may occur.

Blood Glucose: CRITICAL — hypoglycemia must be excluded as cause of encephalopathy (Litchi toxin-related hypoglycemic encephalopathy in Muzaffarpur mimics JE).

Serum Electrolytes: Hyponatremia from SIADH (syndrome of inappropriate ADH) common in encephalitis.

5. Diagnostic Criteria

Confirmed JE: Clinical encephalitis + JE IgM positive in CSF. Probable JE: Clinical encephalitis + JE IgM positive in serum in endemic area during transmission season.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. NO Specific Antiviral for JE — Treatment Is Entirely Supportive

Airway Management: Maintain patent airway. Intubation and mechanical ventilation if GCS ≤8 or respiratory failure. Many JE patients need ICU care.

Seizure Control: IV Lorazepam 0.1mg/kg for acute seizures. IV Phenytoin loading 20mg/kg for recurrent seizures. Levetiracetam as maintenance.

Raised Intracranial Pressure: Mannitol 20% — 0.5-1g/kg IV over 20 minutes every 4-6 hours. Elevate head of bed 30°. Avoid fluid overload.

IV Fluids & Nutrition: Maintain euvolemia. Correct hyponatremia cautiously. Nasogastric feeding if swallowing impaired.

Fever Control: Paracetamol 10-15mg/kg every 4-6 hours. Tepid sponging.

2. Rehabilitation (Critical for Survivors)

Physiotherapy: Early mobilization, passive/active limb exercises for motor deficits. Prevent contractures and pressure sores in bedridden patients.

Speech Therapy: For dysarthria and dysphagia rehabilitation.

Occupational Therapy: Retraining daily living skills. Cognitive rehabilitation for children.

3. Prevention (MOST IMPORTANT)

JE Vaccine (SA 14-14-2): Live attenuated vaccine in India's UIP for endemic districts. 2 doses at 9 months and 16-24 months. Highly effective. Campaign vaccination in outbreak areas.

Vector Control: Reduce rice paddy mosquito breeding, use insecticide-treated nets, indoor residual spraying in endemic areas.

Personal Protection: Use mosquito repellents, sleep under bed nets especially during monsoon in endemic areas.
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Paracetamol Tablets/Syrup: For fever control. Pack of 10/bottle.

ORS Powder: For hydration support.

Phenytoin Tablets IP 100mg: For seizure control. Pack of 10.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):

Paracetamol 500mg, 10's: ₹3.00

Phenytoin 100mg, 10's: ₹4.50
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
IMPORTANT NOTE — No Ayurvedic Cure for JE: Japanese Encephalitis is an acute viral brain infection with 20-30% mortality and 30-50% permanent neurological damage. There is NO effective Ayurvedic treatment for active JE. The only interventions are ICU supportive care and prevention (vaccination).

Guduchi/Giloy (Tinospora cordifolia): May support immune recovery during convalescent phase. 500mg-1g twice daily AFTER acute phase resolved. Not during active encephalitis.

Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera): Neuroprotective Rasayana for post-JE neurological rehabilitation phase. 300-600mg twice daily. May support cognitive recovery.

Brahmi (Bacopa monnieri): Medhya Rasayana for cognitive rehabilitation in JE survivors. 300-500mg daily. Published neuroprotective evidence.

Physiotherapy + Yoga: Gentle yoga and physiotherapy for motor rehabilitation in JE survivors with residual deficits. Only during recovery phase.
diseases.india_specific:
Endemic in Rice-Growing Eastern India: JE is transmitted by Culex tritaeniorhynchus mosquitoes that breed in rice paddies. Endemic in UP, Bihar, Assam, AP, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and West Bengal. Pigs are the amplifying host. Outbreaks peak during and after monsoon (August-October).

Gorakhpur/Eastern UP — Major Hotspot: Gorakhpur division in UP has been the epicenter of devastating annual JE/AES (Acute Encephalitis Syndrome) outbreaks killing hundreds of children. BRD Medical College outbreaks drew national attention.

JE Vaccine in UIP: India included JE vaccine (SA 14-14-2 live attenuated) in the Universal Immunization Programme for endemic districts. Two doses: 9 months and 16-24 months. Has dramatically reduced JE cases in vaccinated areas.

AES — Broader Syndrome: Acute Encephalitis Syndrome in India includes JE plus other causes (Scrub typhus, Enteroviruses, Nipah, Litchi toxin hypoglycemia in Muzaffarpur). Not all AES is JE — differential diagnosis is critical.
diseases.important_notes:
ABSOLUTE CRITICAL — ICU Care Needed: JE is a medical emergency with 20-30% case fatality. Any child/adult with fever + altered consciousness in an endemic area during monsoon must be rushed to a hospital with ICU/ventilator facility. There is NO home treatment for JE.

Vaccination = Only True Prevention: JE vaccine in India's UIP has dramatically reduced cases in vaccinated areas. Ensure every child in endemic districts receives both doses (9 months + 16-24 months). Catch-up vaccination for older children during outbreaks.

Exclude Hypoglycemia First: In children with AES in Bihar/eastern UP (Litchi belt), ALWAYS check blood glucose immediately. Litchi toxin-related hypoglycemic encephalopathy mimics JE but is treatable with IV Dextrose. Delayed glucose correction = death or brain damage.

Ayurvedic Herbs for Recovery Phase Only: Ashwagandha, Brahmi, and Guduchi can support neurological rehabilitation in JE survivors but have NO role during acute infection. Never delay hospital transfer for Ayurvedic treatment.

Long-Term Rehabilitation: 30-50% of JE survivors have permanent neurological sequelae. Families need counseling about long-term rehabilitation needs — physiotherapy, speech therapy, special education for affected children.

diseases.name: Jaundice
diseases.tests:
1. Blood Tests

Serum Bilirubin (Total, Direct, Indirect): Total >1.2 mg/dL = hyperbilirubinemia. Direct (conjugated) >50% = hepatocellular/obstructive. Indirect (unconjugated) predominant = hemolytic/Gilbert's.

Liver Function Tests (AST, ALT, ALP, GGT): ALT/AST >10x = hepatocellular (hepatitis). ALP/GGT disproportionately elevated = obstructive/cholestatic.

Serum Albumin & PT/INR: Synthetic function markers. Low albumin + prolonged PT = poor hepatic reserve.

Complete Blood Count + Reticulocyte Count: Anemia + raised reticulocytes + indirect bilirubin = hemolytic jaundice. Peripheral smear morphology (spherocytes, sickle cells, fragments) identifies cause.

LDH & Haptoglobin: Elevated LDH + low haptoglobin = hemolysis.

2. Viral Markers

HBsAg, Anti-HCV, IgM Anti-HAV, IgM Anti-HEV: To identify viral cause. (See Hepatitis sheet.)

3. Imaging

Ultrasound Abdomen: First-line imaging. Dilated bile ducts = obstructive. Gallstones, liver masses, liver abscess, fatty liver.

MRCP (Magnetic Resonance Cholangiopancreatography): Non-invasive visualization of biliary tree for obstructive jaundice. Identifies CBD stones, strictures, tumors.

ERCP: Therapeutic — stone extraction, stent placement for obstructive jaundice. Both diagnostic and therapeutic.

4. Neonatal Jaundice

Transcutaneous Bilirubinometer (TcB): Non-invasive screening tool for neonatal jaundice. Correlates with serum bilirubin.

Serum Total Bilirubin: Plotted on Bhutani Nomogram (hour-specific) to determine risk zone and need for phototherapy/exchange transfusion.

5. Key Classification

Pre-hepatic (Hemolytic): Unconjugated bilirubin elevated. Causes: hemolysis (G6PD, sickle cell, thalassemia, malaria). Hepatic (Hepatocellular): Both conjugated and unconjugated elevated. Causes: hepatitis, cirrhosis, drugs. Post-hepatic (Obstructive): Conjugated bilirubin elevated. Causes: gallstones, tumors, strictures.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. Treat Underlying Cause (Disease-Specific)

Hepatocellular: Treat hepatitis (see Hepatitis sheet), stop hepatotoxic drugs, manage cirrhosis.

Obstructive: ERCP for stone extraction, stenting for tumors, surgery for malignancy.

Hemolytic: Treat underlying cause (stop offending drug, steroids for autoimmune hemolysis, transfusion for sickle cell crisis).

2. Neonatal Jaundice Treatment

Phototherapy: Blue-spectrum light (wavelength 460-490nm) converts bilirubin to water-soluble isomers excreted without conjugation. Started based on Bhutani Nomogram thresholds. Available from district hospital level. MOST babies respond to phototherapy alone.

Exchange Transfusion: For severe neonatal jaundice not responding to intensive phototherapy (bilirubin approaching exchange threshold) or signs of acute bilirubin encephalopathy. Removes bilirubin-loaded blood and replaces with donor blood.

3. Symptomatic Relief

Cholestyramine/Ursodeoxycholic Acid: For pruritus in obstructive/cholestatic jaundice. Ursodiol 10-15mg/kg/day for cholestatic pruritus.

Antihistamines (Hydroxyzine): For symptomatic itch relief. 25mg at bedtime.

4. Supportive

Adequate nutrition, avoid hepatotoxins (alcohol, unnecessary Paracetamol), manage complications (ascites, encephalopathy in cirrhosis).
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Paracetamol Tablets IP 500mg: Use cautiously — max 2g/day in liver disease.

ORS Powder: Hydration support.

Ursodeoxycholic Acid Tablets IP 300mg: For cholestatic pruritus.

Iron + Folic Acid Tablets: For hemolytic anemia.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):

Paracetamol 500mg, 10's: ₹3.00

Ursodeoxycholic Acid 300mg, 10's: ₹65.00
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Bhumyamalaki (Phyllanthus niruri): Premier Ayurvedic hepatoprotective for Kamala (jaundice). Published evidence for anti-HBV and hepatoprotective activity. 500mg-1g twice daily.

Kutki (Picrorhiza kurroa): Classical Yakrit Shodhaka. Kutkin compound hepatoprotective. 500mg-1g twice daily.

Arogyavardhini Vati: Classical for Kamala (jaundice). 2 tablets twice daily. Contains Kutki, Triphala, Loha Bhasma.

Kalmegh (Andrographis paniculata): Hepatoprotective and Jwarahara. 500mg-1g twice daily.

Sugarcane Juice (Ikshu Rasa): Traditional Indian remedy for jaundice. Cooling, Pittashamaka, easily digestible energy source. 200-300ml twice daily.

Radish Juice (Mulaka Rasa): Traditional liver stimulant. Cholagogue — promotes bile flow. 30-50ml on empty stomach.

Himalaya Liv.52: India's most popular hepatoprotective. 2 tablets twice daily.

Dabur Arogyavardhini Vati: Branded classical preparation.
diseases.india_specific:
Hepatitis-Endemic Country: Viral hepatitis (A, B, C, E) is the most common cause of jaundice in India. Hep A/E from contaminated water; Hep B/C from blood/needles. (See Hepatitis sheet for details.)

Neonatal Jaundice — Leading Cause of Preventable Brain Damage: India has the highest global burden of severe neonatal jaundice. Kernicterus (bilirubin brain damage) from untreated severe neonatal jaundice causes permanent cerebral palsy, deafness, and intellectual disability. Early phototherapy is life-saving.

Hemolytic Jaundice from G6PD Deficiency & Sickle Cell: G6PD deficiency is prevalent in tribal India (10-15%). Certain drugs (Primaquine, Dapsone, Nitrofurantoin) and foods (fava beans) trigger hemolytic crises with jaundice. Sickle cell disease prevalent in tribal belt (MP, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Maharashtra).

Obstructive Jaundice from Gallstones: Gallstone disease is common in India (especially North Indian women). Choledocholithiasis (CBD stone) causes obstructive jaundice requiring ERCP or surgery.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — Identify the Type First: Jaundice is a SYMPTOM, not a disease. Treatment depends entirely on cause — hemolytic (blood), hepatocellular (liver), or obstructive (bile duct). Always investigate with LFTs, bilirubin fractions, ultrasound, and viral markers before starting treatment.

Neonatal Jaundice — Phototherapy Saves Brains: Every hour of delay in treating severe neonatal jaundice increases risk of kernicterus (permanent brain damage). If a newborn appears yellow, seek medical evaluation IMMEDIATELY. Phototherapy is safe and widely available.

Ayurvedic Kamala Treatment — Supportive: Bhumyamalaki, Kutki, and Arogyavardhini Vati are excellent hepatoprotective adjuncts for hepatocellular jaundice but CANNOT treat obstructive jaundice (needs ERCP/surgery) or hemolytic jaundice (needs cause-specific treatment).

Sugarcane Juice — Traditional but Genuinely Helpful: Sugarcane juice is a traditional Indian jaundice remedy with genuine nutritional merit — provides easily digestible calories and keeps the patient hydrated. Safe to encourage alongside medical treatment.

AVOID Hepatotoxins: During jaundice, strictly avoid alcohol, unnecessary Paracetamol (max 2g/day), and any herbal preparations not prescribed by qualified practitioners. The damaged liver cannot process toxins.
confirmation_tests_curated: Serum Bilirubin (Total, Direct, Indirect): Total >1.2 mg/dL = hyperbilirubinemia. Direct (conjugated) >50% = hepatocellular/obstructive. Indirect (unconjugated) predominant = hemolytic/Gilbert's.
Liver Function Tests (AST, ALT, ALP, GGT): ALT/AST >10x = hepatocellular (hepatitis). ALP/GGT disproportionately elevated = obstructive/cholestatic.
Serum Albumin & PT/INR: Synthetic function markers. Low albumin + prolonged PT = poor hepatic reserve.

Complete Blood Count + Reticulocyte Count: Anemia + raised reticulocytes + indirect bilirubin = hemolytic jaundice. Peripheral smear morphology (spherocytes, sickle cells, fragments) identifies cause.

diseases.name: Kala-Azar
diseases.tests:
1. Serological Rapid Test (First-Line in India)

rK39 Rapid Immunochromatographic Test: Point-of-care finger-prick test detecting antibodies against rK39 antigen of L. donovani. Results in 15-20 minutes. Sensitivity >95%, Specificity >90% in Indian subcontinent. NVBDCP-recommended first-line diagnostic for India. Available at PHC level.

2. Parasitological Tests

Splenic Aspirate: Highest sensitivity (95-98%) — demonstrates LD bodies (amastigotes) on Giemsa stain. But carries 0.1% risk of fatal hemorrhage. Requires trained personnel. Not done if platelets <40,000.

Bone Marrow Aspirate: Sensitivity 52-85%. Safer than splenic aspirate. Demonstrates LD bodies. Available at district hospital level.

3. Blood Tests

Complete Blood Count: Pancytopenia — anemia + leukopenia + thrombocytopenia. Reversed albumin:globulin ratio (high globulin, low albumin). Very high ESR.

Serum Protein Electrophoresis: Polyclonal hypergammaglobulinemia — characteristic. Aldehyde/Formol Gel Test (positive due to high globulin) — crude but historical field test.

4. Diagnostic Criteria (NVBDCP India)

Clinically suspected: Fever >2 weeks + splenomegaly in endemic area. Confirmed: Clinical suspicion + positive rK39 test OR demonstration of LD bodies on tissue aspirate. In Bihar endemic districts, positive rK39 in a patient with >2 weeks fever and splenomegaly is sufficient to start treatment.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. First-Line Treatment (India)

Liposomal Amphotericin B (Single Dose): 10mg/kg single IV infusion. Current NVBDCP recommended first-line for India. 95-98% cure rate. Single-dose protocol makes it most practical for programmatic use. Administered at district hospital level.

2. Alternative Treatments

Miltefosine (Oral): 50mg twice daily (adults >25kg) for 28 days. First oral anti-leishmanial. Decreasing efficacy due to resistance in Bihar (~85% cure rate now). Contraindicated in pregnancy (teratogenic).

Amphotericin B Deoxycholate (IV): 1mg/kg alternate days for 15 infusions. Older formulation. Effective but nephrotoxic, requires prolonged hospitalization. Still used where liposomal form unavailable.

Paromomycin (IM): 11mg/kg IM daily for 21 days. Alternative in combination regimens.

3. PKDL Treatment

Miltefosine for 12 weeks OR Liposomal Amphotericin B 5mg/kg × 4 doses. PKDL treatment is critical for interrupting community transmission.

4. Prevention (NVBDCP Elimination Strategy)

Indoor Residual Spraying (IRS): DDT/synthetic pyrethroid spraying of houses in endemic areas. Key vector control measure. Two rounds per year.

Insecticide-Treated Bed Nets (ITN): Long-lasting insecticidal nets. Distribute in endemic villages.

Case Detection & Treatment: Active case search by ASHAs. Early rK39 testing and treatment. Free treatment under NVBDCP.
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Liposomal Amphotericin B: Provided FREE under NVBDCP programme. Not commercially sold at Jan Aushadhi.

Miltefosine Capsules 50mg: Available through NVBDCP. Pack of 28.

Iron + Folic Acid Tablets: For Kala-azar-related anemia.

Paracetamol Tablets IP 500mg: For fever. Pack of 10.

ORS Powder: Hydration support.

Note: All Kala-azar treatment is provided FREE by the Government of India through NVBDCP at designated treatment centres.
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Guduchi/Giloy (Tinospora cordifolia): Immunomodulatory Rasayana. Published research on anti-leishmanial activity of tinosporin compounds. May support immune recovery post-treatment. 500mg-1g twice daily.

Neem (Azadirachta indica): Published in-vitro anti-leishmanial activity. Traditional insect repellent — neem oil application may help reduce sandfly bites. Neem leaves burned as fumigant.

Pippali (Piper longum): Piperine has shown leishmanicidal activity in research. Used as Rasayana. Part of Trikatu.

Ashwagandha: Immune recovery support post-treatment. 300-600mg twice daily.

Chyawanprash: Rasayana for general immunity and strength recovery. 10-20g daily with warm milk.
diseases.india_specific:
Bihar — Epicenter: India accounts for ~50% of global Kala-azar cases. Bihar alone reports 80-90% of Indian cases. Jharkhand, West Bengal, and UP are also endemic. India targets elimination (<1 case per 10,000 population) by 2023 (extended to 2026).

Sandfly Vector (Phlebotomus argentipes): Transmitted by the bite of female Phlebotomus sandflies. Sandflies breed in cool, dark, humid environments — mud/brick walls with cracks, animal sheds, dark rooms. Bite at dusk/night.

Post-Kala-azar Dermal Leishmaniasis (PKDL): 5-10% of treated Kala-azar patients develop PKDL — macular/papular/nodular skin lesions appearing months to years after treatment. PKDL patients serve as reservoir, maintaining community transmission.

Poverty & Housing: Kala-azar is a disease of the poorest — mud houses, poor ventilation, sleeping on floor, proximity to cattle sheds. Improved housing is a key elimination strategy.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — Free Government Treatment: ALL Kala-azar treatment is provided FREE by the Government of India through NVBDCP at PHC/CHC/District Hospital level. Patients should NOT buy expensive private treatment. Single-dose Liposomal Amphotericin B cures 95%+ cases.

Complete Treatment Prevents PKDL: Incomplete treatment leads to relapse and PKDL (skin lesions that maintain community transmission). Complete the full prescribed course. Follow up at 6 months to confirm cure.

Ayurveda Cannot Treat Active Kala-azar: Kala-azar is caused by Leishmania donovani parasite and is FATAL if untreated (mortality >95%). Ayurvedic herbs cannot kill the parasite. Guduchi/Neem may support recovery AFTER standard treatment, not replace it.

IRS + Bed Nets Save Communities: Allow indoor residual spraying teams to spray ALL rooms. Sleep under insecticide-treated bed nets. These two measures dramatically reduce sandfly transmission.

Report Every Case: Kala-azar is a notifiable disease. Every case must be reported to NVBDCP through Kala-azar Management Information System (KAMIS). This is essential for India's elimination target.

diseases.name: Kidney Disease
diseases.tests:
1. Urine Tests (Screening & Diagnosis)

Urine Routine & Microscopy: Proteinuria, hematuria, casts (RBC casts = glomerulonephritis, granular casts = tubular damage). First screening test.

Urine Albumin-to-Creatinine Ratio (UACR): Spot urine test. Microalbuminuria (30-300 mg/g) = early kidney damage. Macroalbuminuria (>300) = established nephropathy. ICMR recommends annual screening for all diabetics and hypertensives.

24-Hour Urine Protein: Quantifies total protein excretion. >3.5g/day = nephrotic range proteinuria.

2. Blood Tests

Serum Creatinine & eGFR: Serum creatinine elevated when kidney function significantly impaired. eGFR (calculated from creatinine, age, sex) is the primary CKD staging measure. CKD-EPI formula preferred.

Blood Urea Nitrogen (BUN): Elevated in renal failure. BUN:Creatinine ratio helps identify pre-renal vs intrinsic causes.

Serum Electrolytes (Na+, K+, Ca2+, PO4): Hyperkalemia (life-threatening), hyperphosphatemia, hypocalcemia in advanced CKD. Guide dietary and medication adjustments.

Complete Blood Count: Normocytic normochromic anemia (EPO deficiency). Hemoglobin <10 g/dL in many CKD stage 4-5 patients.

Serum Albumin: Low albumin indicates nephrotic syndrome or malnutrition. Prognostic marker in CKD.

iPTH (Parathyroid Hormone): Secondary hyperparathyroidism in CKD-MBD (mineral bone disease). Elevated PTH causes bone resorption.

HbA1c & Fasting Glucose: Optimize diabetic control. HbA1c unreliable in advanced CKD (altered RBC lifespan) — use fructosamine.

3. Imaging

Ultrasound Kidneys: Bilateral small kidneys (<9cm) = chronic disease. Normal/large kidneys = acute or early CKD, diabetic nephropathy, ADPKD, amyloidosis. Cortical thinning, loss of corticomedullary differentiation.

4. Kidney Biopsy

Renal Biopsy: Definitive diagnosis of glomerular diseases (IgA nephropathy, membranous, FSGS, lupus nephritis). Done under USG guidance. Indicated when etiology unclear and biopsy will change management.

5. CKD Staging (KDIGO)

Stage 1: eGFR ≥90 with kidney damage markers. Stage 2: 60-89. Stage 3a: 45-59. Stage 3b: 30-44. Stage 4: 15-29. Stage 5 (ESRD): <15 or on dialysis. Combined with albuminuria category (A1/A2/A3) for risk stratification.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. Slow Progression (All CKD Stages)

ACE Inhibitors/ARBs: Enalapril, Ramipril, Telmisartan, Losartan. Renoprotective — reduce proteinuria and slow CKD progression. First-line for ALL CKD patients with proteinuria/diabetes/hypertension. Titrate to maximum tolerated dose.

SGLT2 Inhibitors (Dapagliflozin/Empagliflozin): LANDMARK drugs — now recommended for ALL CKD with eGFR >20 regardless of diabetes. Reduce CKD progression by 30-40% (DAPA-CKD, EMPA-KIDNEY trials). Game-changing for Indian CKD management.

Blood Pressure Control: Target <130/80 mmHg. Use ACEi/ARB + Amlodipine + Diuretic as needed. Strict BP control slows CKD progression significantly.

Glycemic Control: HbA1c target 7-8% in CKD (avoid hypoglycemia). Metformin dose-adjusted based on eGFR (stop if <30). SGLT2i preferred.

2. Complication Management

Erythropoietin (EPO) + Iron: For CKD anemia. EPO 4000-10000 IU SC weekly + IV Iron Sucrose. Target Hb 10-11.5 g/dL. Avoid Hb >13 (increases cardiovascular risk).

Phosphate Binders (Calcium Carbonate/Sevelamer): For hyperphosphatemia. Take with meals. Reduce dietary phosphate.

Vitamin D (Calcitriol/Alfacalcidol): For secondary hyperparathyroidism. 0.25-0.5 mcg daily.

Sodium Bicarbonate: For metabolic acidosis (HCO3 <22). 500-1000mg thrice daily.

3. Renal Replacement Therapy (ESRD — Stage 5)

Hemodialysis: 3 sessions/week, 4 hours each. Available at PM National Dialysis Programme centres (free at district hospitals). Requires AV fistula creation 3-6 months before starting.

Peritoneal Dialysis (CAPD): Home-based. Patient performs 3-4 exchanges/day. More autonomy, suitable for rural patients. Under-utilized in India.

Kidney Transplant: Best long-term option. Live-related donor preferred. 5-year graft survival >85%. Transplantation of Human Organs Act regulates organ donation in India.
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Enalapril Tablets IP 5mg: ACEi for CKD. Pack of 10.

Telmisartan Tablets IP 40mg: ARB for CKD. Pack of 10.

Amlodipine Tablets IP 5mg: For BP control in CKD. Pack of 10.

Calcium Carbonate Tablets: Phosphate binder. Pack of 10.

Iron + Folic Acid Tablets: For CKD anemia support.

Sodium Bicarbonate Tablets: For metabolic acidosis.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):

Enalapril 5mg, 10's: ₹8.00

Telmisartan 40mg, 10's: ₹12.00

Amlodipine 5mg, 10's: ₹3.50

Calcium Carbonate 500mg, 10's: ₹5.00
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Punarnava (Boerhaavia diffusa): Classical Mutrala (diuretic) and Shothaghna (anti-edema). Published research shows renoprotective activity. The most important Ayurvedic herb for kidney conditions. 3-5g powder or Punarnavadi Kwatha twice daily.

Gokshura (Tribulus terrestris): Classical Mutravirechaniya and Mutrala. Supports kidney function and urine formation. 3-5g powder twice daily. Published nephroprotective evidence.

Chandraprabha Vati: Classical formulation for Mutraroga (urinary diseases) and Prameha (diabetes). Contains Shilajeet, Guggulu, and 37 ingredients. 2 tablets twice daily.

Varunadi Kashaya: Contains Varuna (Crataeva nurvala) and Punarnava. Classical for urinary and kidney conditions.

Shilajeet (Asphaltum): Rasayana with published renoprotective and antioxidant properties. Contains fulvic acid. 250-500mg twice daily.

Mutrakricchhantak Churna (Planet Ayurveda): Proprietary blend of Gokshura, Punarnava, Varuna, and other kidney herbs.

Himalaya Cystone: Proprietary for urinary health. 2 tablets twice daily.

Patanjali Divya Gokshuradi Guggulu: For urinary and kidney support.
diseases.india_specific:
Diabetes & Hypertension — Leading Causes: Diabetic nephropathy and hypertensive nephrosclerosis cause 50-60% of CKD in India. India's twin epidemics of diabetes (101M) and hypertension (220M) directly drive the CKD epidemic.

Massive Undiagnosed Burden: India has an estimated 17-20 crore people with some stage of CKD. Majority undiagnosed. Only 10-15% of ESRD patients receive any form of renal replacement therapy (dialysis/transplant).

Dialysis Access Gap: India has approximately 5,000 dialysis centres vs estimated need for 50,000+. PM National Dialysis Programme (PMNDP) provides free dialysis at district hospitals — expanding but still insufficient for rural India.

Ayurvedic Nephrotoxicity Risk: Certain Ayurvedic preparations containing heavy metals (lead, mercury, arsenic in Bhasmas) can cause acute kidney injury. This is a significant India-specific concern — only use certified, standardized Ayurvedic products.

Contrast Nephropathy & NSAID Use: Widespread use of IV contrast (CT scans) without pre-hydration and chronic NSAID use (OTC Ibuprofen/Diclofenac) for pain are preventable causes of kidney damage in India.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — CKD Is Irreversible: Once kidney function is lost, it cannot be regenerated. Treatment focuses on SLOWING progression. ACEi/ARB + SGLT2 inhibitor + BP control + diabetes control is the evidence-based approach. Ayurvedic herbs support but CANNOT reverse CKD.

BEWARE Ayurvedic Heavy Metal Nephrotoxicity: Some traditional Bhasma preparations (containing lead, mercury, arsenic) can CAUSE kidney damage. Only use standardized, certified Ayurvedic products from reputable manufacturers. If kidney function is already impaired, consult a nephrologist before taking any Ayurvedic preparations.

Punarnava & Gokshura — Genuine Renal Support: These herbs have published evidence for diuretic and nephroprotective activity. They can safely complement standard CKD management (with doctor's knowledge) but cannot replace ACEi/ARBs, SGLT2 inhibitors, or dialysis.

Screen All Diabetics & Hypertensives Annually: Annual UACR and serum creatinine/eGFR testing for ALL diabetics and hypertensive patients can detect CKD early when progression can be slowed. This simple screening is the most impactful intervention.

PM National Dialysis Programme: Free hemodialysis is available at district hospitals under PMNDP for ESRD patients. Educate patients about this government programme. AV fistula creation should be planned early (eGFR <20) to avoid emergency dialysis catheter.

diseases.name: Leprosy
diseases.tests:
1. Clinical Diagnosis (Primary Method)

Cardinal Signs of Leprosy (WHO): Diagnosis requires ≥1 of: (a) Hypopigmented/reddish skin patch with definite loss of sensation, (b) Thickened peripheral nerve, (c) Acid-fast bacilli on skin-slit smear. Clinical diagnosis by trained health worker is sufficient to start MDT — NO lab confirmation needed at PHC level.

Skin Examination: Map all patches noting number, size, location, borders (well/ill-defined), surface (smooth/rough), sensation (test with cotton wisp/pin prick/hot-cold). Classify: Paucibacillary (PB: 1-5 patches) vs Multibacillary (MB: >5 patches or any nerve involvement).

Nerve Assessment: Palpate bilateral ulnar, lateral popliteal, greater auricular, radial cutaneous, posterior tibial nerves. Note thickening, tenderness. Test motor function: grip strength, finger abduction, wrist/foot dorsiflexion, eye closure.

2. Laboratory Tests (When Available)

Skin-Slit Smear (SSS): Slit skin from earlobes, patches, and elbows. Stain with Ziehl-Neelsen for Acid-Fast Bacilli (AFB). Calculate Bacteriological Index (BI 0-6+). Positive in MB leprosy. Available at district leprosy centres.

Skin Biopsy with Histopathology: Punch biopsy from active patch edge. Histopathology shows granulomas (tuberculoid) or foamy macrophages with AFB (lepromatous). Ridley-Jopling classification: TT-BT-BB-BL-LL spectrum. Available at medical college/reference labs.

3. Additional Tests

Nerve Conduction Studies (NCS): Quantifies nerve damage. Slowed conduction velocity in affected nerves. Useful for monitoring neuritis treatment. Tertiary centre availability.

Voluntary Muscle Testing (VMT) & Sensory Testing (ST): Standardized grading of motor and sensory function at each visit. WHO disability grading: Grade 0 (no disability), Grade 1 (sensory loss), Grade 2 (visible deformity/damage).

4. Key Diagnostic Criteria (WHO/NLEP)

Leprosy is diagnosed CLINICALLY: Anesthetic skin patch + thick nerve = leprosy. PB leprosy: 1-5 skin patches, no/1 nerve, smear negative. MB leprosy: >5 patches, or >1 nerve, or smear positive. Treatment classification (PB vs MB) guides MDT regimen. In India, ASHA/HW-trained skin examination is the primary case-finding strategy.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. Multi-Drug Therapy (MDT) — WHO Standard Regimen

PB Leprosy MDT (6 months): Rifampicin 600mg once monthly (supervised) + Dapsone 100mg daily (self-administered). Duration: 6 months. Cure rate >98%.

MB Leprosy MDT (12 months): Rifampicin 600mg + Clofazimine 300mg once monthly (supervised) + Dapsone 100mg + Clofazimine 50mg daily (self-administered). Duration: 12 months. Cure rate >98%.

Single Lesion PB: Single dose ROM (Rifampicin 600mg + Ofloxacin 400mg + Minocycline 100mg). Alternative for single-lesion PB leprosy.

2. Reaction Management (EMERGENCY)

Prednisolone (Type 1 Reaction & Neuritis): Start 40-60mg daily, taper over 12-20 weeks. CRITICAL for preventing permanent nerve damage. Do NOT stop abruptly. Every leprosy health worker must be able to start steroids for reactions.

Prednisolone + Thalidomide (Severe ENL/Type 2): Thalidomide 100-300mg daily (CONTRAINDICATED in women of childbearing age — teratogenic). Prednisolone 40mg tapered. Clofazimine 300mg daily for chronic ENL.

3. Disability Prevention & Management

Self-Care Education: Daily soaking of anesthetic hands/feet in water, oiling, wound inspection, use of protective footwear (MCR chappals — Microcellular Rubber footwear provided free by NLEP). Prevents ulcers and secondary infection.

Reconstructive Surgery: Tendon transfer for claw hand, foot drop correction, lagophthalmos correction. Available at NLEP-designated hospitals. Restores function and reduces stigma.

4. Programme Support

All MDT drugs provided FREE by NLEP through PHC/subcenter. No cost to patient. MDT blister packs (calendar packs) are supplied by WHO through NLEP. Active case-finding through ASHA-led Sparsh Leprosy Awareness Campaign (SLAC) and Leprosy Case Detection Campaign (LCDC).
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Prednisolone Tablets IP 5mg: For leprosy reactions/neuritis. Pack of 10.

Dapsone Tablets IP 100mg: Component of MDT. Available at Jan Aushadhi stores.

Paracetamol Tablets IP 500mg: For pain and fever during reactions. Pack of 10.

Clotrimazole Cream 1%: For fungal co-infection of trophic ulcers.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):

Prednisolone 5mg, 10's: ₹3.50

Dapsone 100mg, 10's: ₹5.00

Note: Complete MDT blister packs provided FREE by NLEP — patients need not purchase.
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Bakuchi / Babchi (Psoralea corylifolia): Classical herb for Kushtha (skin diseases) in Ayurveda. Contains psoralen — used historically for repigmentation of hypopigmented patches. Topical oil application on patches. Published evidence for anti-mycobacterial activity. 3-5g powder twice daily or Bakuchi taila external.

Khadira (Acacia catechu): Classical Kushthahara (anti-dermatosis) herb. Heartwood decoction used for chronic skin conditions. Part of Khadirarishta. Anti-inflammatory and wound healing.

Tuvaraka / Chaulmoogra (Hydnocarpus wightiana): Historical treatment for leprosy for centuries before MDT. Chaulmoogra oil was THE standard leprosy treatment in pre-antibiotic era. Contains hydnocarpic acid with anti-mycobacterial activity. Now largely replaced by MDT but of historical significance.

Guduchi/Giloy (Tinospora cordifolia): Immunomodulatory Rasayana to support recovery and prevent reactions. 500mg-1g twice daily.

Neem (Azadirachta indica): Classical Kushthahara. Neem oil for external application on ulcers. Neem decoction for wound washing. Antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory.

Arogyavardhini Vati: Classical formulation for skin diseases and liver support. 2 tablets twice daily.

Mahamanjishthadi Kwatha: Classical blood purifier and Kushthahara formulation containing Manjistha, Guduchi, Triphala. 15-30ml twice daily.

Panchatikta Ghrita Guggulu: Classical for Kushtha Roga. Contains Neem, Guduchi, Patola, Kantakari, Vasa in ghee-Guggulu base. 2 tablets twice daily. Specific for chronic skin conditions.
diseases.india_specific:
India — Largest Global Burden: India accounts for ~55-60% of new leprosy cases globally (~100,000+ new cases/year). 'Elimination' (prevalence <1/10,000) was declared in 2005 at national level, but many districts remain high-endemic, especially in Bihar, UP, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Odisha, Maharashtra, and West Bengal.

Child Cases Indicate Ongoing Transmission: India still detects 8-10% of new leprosy cases in children under 15 years — indicating active community transmission despite 'elimination' claims. This is a programme concern.

Grade 2 Disability at Detection: ~3-4% of new cases in India are detected with visible deformities (Grade 2 disability), indicating delayed diagnosis. Active case-finding (ASHA-led skin camps) is essential to detect cases earlier.

Leprosy Colonies & Rehabilitation: India has ~800+ leprosy colonies housing patients and families affected by historical social exclusion. Rehabilitation through NLEP and NGO partnerships provides livelihood support, reconstructive surgery, and community reintegration.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — MDT Cures Leprosy: Multi-Drug Therapy completely cures leprosy in 6-12 months. MDT is provided FREE by the Government. There is NO reason to delay or avoid treatment. Leprosy is CURABLE. Untreated leprosy causes irreversible disability.

Leprosy Is NOT Highly Contagious: 95% of humans have natural immunity. Leprosy requires prolonged close contact for transmission, and patients on MDT become non-infectious within days. Family members should NOT be isolated or stigmatized.

Reactions Are Emergencies — Start Steroids: Type 1 reactions and neuritis cause PERMANENT nerve damage if not treated with Prednisolone within 24-48 hours. Health workers must recognize reaction signs (sudden pain/swelling in patches or nerves) and start steroids immediately without waiting for specialist referral.

Ayurvedic Support for Healing: Bakuchi, Khadira, and Neem have genuine traditional use in Kushtha (skin disease) and can support wound healing and skin recovery alongside MDT. They CANNOT replace MDT antibiotics for killing M. leprae.

Fight Stigma — End Discrimination: Leprosy-related stigma is the biggest barrier to early diagnosis and treatment in India. ASHA workers and communities must be educated: leprosy is just a curable bacterial infection, not a curse or punishment. Discrimination against leprosy patients is illegal under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act 2016.

diseases.name: Measles
diseases.tests:
1. Clinical Diagnosis (Primary Method)

Clinical Recognition: Measles is diagnosed CLINICALLY in most cases: Fever + cough/coryza/conjunctivitis + characteristic cephalocaudal maculopapular rash. Koplik's spots (if seen) are confirmatory before rash. Lab confirmation needed for surveillance but NOT for treatment initiation.

2. Laboratory Confirmation (For Surveillance)

Measles IgM ELISA (Serum): Positive from day 3-4 of rash onset. Remains positive for 30-60 days. Gold standard serological test. Collected at sentinel surveillance sites.

Measles RNA RT-PCR: Throat swab or urine. Confirms active infection and enables genotyping for outbreak tracking. Available at WHO-SEARO reference labs.

3. Routine Blood Tests

Complete Blood Count: Leukopenia with lymphopenia typical. Leukocytosis suggests secondary bacterial infection (pneumonia).

4. Complication Assessment

Chest X-ray: For suspected measles pneumonia — shows bilateral infiltrates (viral) or lobar consolidation (secondary bacterial).

CSF Analysis: If encephalitis suspected — lymphocytic pleocytosis, elevated protein.

5. Key Diagnostic Criteria (WHO)

Suspected Measles: Any person with fever + generalized maculopapular rash + one of (cough, coryza, conjunctivitis). Confirmed: Suspected case + positive measles IgM or epidemiological link to confirmed case. Every suspected case must be reported to IDSP for outbreak investigation.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. NO Specific Antiviral — Treatment Is Supportive

Vitamin A Supplementation (CRITICAL): ALL measles cases must receive Vitamin A. <6 months: 50,000 IU. 6-11 months: 100,000 IU. ≥12 months: 200,000 IU. Two doses — day 1 and day 2. Reduces mortality by 50% and prevents blindness. WHO/IMNCI mandatory protocol.

Paracetamol: For fever and discomfort. 10-15mg/kg every 4-6 hours. Avoid Aspirin (Reye's syndrome risk).

Oral Rehydration: ORS for diarrhea-associated dehydration. Encourage breast milk and fluids.

Nutritional Support: Continue feeding/breastfeeding. Extra meal daily for 2 weeks after recovery (catch-up nutrition). Measles causes acute malnutrition.

2. Complication Management

Antibiotics for Pneumonia: Amoxicillin 45mg/kg/day in 2 doses for 5 days (non-severe). Ceftriaxone + Azithromycin IV for severe measles pneumonia. Refer to hospital.

Antibiotics for Otitis Media: Amoxicillin 45mg/kg/day for 5 days.

Eye Care: Vitamin A + Tetracycline eye ointment for corneal ulceration. Protect eyes from light. Refer to ophthalmologist if corneal involvement.

Seizure Management: Diazepam/Lorazepam for measles-related seizures. Evaluate for encephalitis if persistent.

3. Prevention (MOST IMPORTANT)

MR Vaccine (Measles-Rubella): Part of India's UIP. MR1 at 9-12 months + MR2 at 16-24 months. Live attenuated vaccine. >95% effective after 2 doses. Every child must be vaccinated — this is the ONLY way to prevent measles.

Post-Exposure Prophylaxis: MR vaccine within 72 hours of exposure for unvaccinated contacts >6 months. Immunoglobulin (IG) within 6 days for high-risk contacts (infants <6 months, immunocompromised, pregnant).

Outbreak Response: Ring vaccination of all unvaccinated children in affected area. Isolation of cases for 4 days after rash onset.
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:
Paracetamol Tablets/Syrup: For fever. Pack of 10 / bottle.

ORS Powder: For diarrhea-related dehydration.

Amoxicillin Capsules IP 250/500mg: For secondary bacterial complications.

Vitamin A Capsules (as per age): Provided free under IMNCI/Vitamin A supplementation programme.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):

Paracetamol 500mg, 10's: ₹3.00

Amoxicillin 250mg, 10's: ₹8.00

ORS sachet: ₹3.50

Note: Vitamin A capsules and MR vaccine provided FREE under government programmes.
diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
IMPORTANT NOTE — No Ayurvedic Cure for Measles: Measles is a highly contagious viral disease. There is no effective Ayurvedic treatment to kill the measles virus. The ONLY prevention is vaccination. Supportive Ayurvedic care focuses on managing symptoms and boosting recovery:

Guduchi/Giloy (Tinospora cordifolia): Immunomodulatory Rasayana for recovery support. 500mg-1g twice daily after acute phase.

Tulsi (Ocimum sanctum): Antipyretic and immune support. Tulsi decoction 2-3 times daily for fever management.

Neem Water Bath: Gentle bathing with neem-infused lukewarm water to soothe skin during rash resolution. Antimicrobial. Traditional Indian practice.

Chyawanprash: Rasayana for post-measles immune recovery. 10-20g daily with warm milk after acute phase resolves.

Coconut Oil Application: Gentle application on desquamating skin (post-rash healing phase) to moisturize and soothe. Do NOT apply during active rash phase.

Light Easily Digestible Diet (Pathya): Moong dal khichdi, rice kanji, fruit juice, coconut water. Avoid heavy, oily, spicy food during illness. Continue breastfeeding in infants.
diseases.india_specific:
India — Largest Measles Burden: India accounted for ~25% of global measles deaths pre-vaccination scale-up. Despite significant progress (MR vaccine campaign covering 324 million children), outbreaks continue in under-vaccinated pockets — tribal areas, urban slums, migrant populations.

Malnutrition Amplifies Severity: Measles in malnourished Indian children is far more severe — higher complication rates (pneumonia, diarrhea), prolonged illness, and higher mortality. Vitamin A deficiency specifically worsens outcomes.

MR Vaccine Campaign: India conducted one of the world's largest vaccination campaigns (2017-2020) — Measles-Rubella (MR) vaccine for 324+ million children aged 9 months to 15 years. Two-dose schedule: MR1 at 9 months + MR2 at 16-24 months (in UIP).

SSPE — Devastating Late Complication: Subacute Sclerosing Panencephalitis (SSPE) is a fatal progressive brain disease appearing 7-10 years after measles infection. India has one of highest SSPE rates globally. Entirely preventable by vaccination.
diseases.important_notes:
ABSOLUTE CRITICAL — Vitamin A Saves Lives: Every child with measles MUST receive Vitamin A — it reduces mortality by 50% and prevents blindness. Two doses given on Day 1 and Day 2. This is the single most important intervention after supportive care. Never miss this.

VACCINATION = ONLY Prevention: Measles has NO cure. The ONLY way to prevent it is MR vaccination. Two doses (9 months + 16-24 months) provide >95% lifelong protection. Every unvaccinated child is at risk. Fight vaccine hesitancy — measles kills, vaccines don't.

Measles Causes 'Immune Amnesia': After measles, the immune system 'forgets' previous infections — making the child vulnerable to pneumonia, diarrhea, and other infections for 2-3 YEARS. This is why measles vaccination prevents deaths from many other diseases too.

Isolate and Report: Measles is a notifiable disease. Every case must be reported to IDSP/WHO surveillance. Isolate patient for 4 days after rash onset. Identify and vaccinate all contacts within 72 hours.

Ayurvedic Support for Recovery Only: Guduchi, Tulsi, and Chyawanprash support post-measles immune recovery. They have NO role in preventing or treating active measles. Never delay hospital referral for complications (pneumonia, encephalitis, severe dehydration) for Ayurvedic treatment.
confirmation_tests_curated: Clinical Recognition: Measles is diagnosed CLINICALLY in most cases: Fever + cough/coryza/conjunctivitis + characteristic cephalocaudal maculopapular rash. Koplik's spots (if seen) are confirmatory before rash. Lab confirmation needed for surveillance but NOT for treatment initiation.
Measles IgM ELISA (Serum): Positive from day 3-4 of rash onset. Remains positive for 30-60 days. Gold standard serological test. Collected at sentinel surveillance sites.
Measles RNA RT-PCR: Throat swab or urine. Confirms active infection and enables genotyping for outbreak tracking. Available at WHO-SEARO reference labs.

diseases.name: Ringworm
diseases.tests:
1. Clinical Diagnosis (Usually Sufficient)

Clinical Recognition: Dermatophytosis is diagnosed CLINICALLY in most cases by the characteristic annular lesion with active scaly border and central clearing. Wood's lamp (UV) fluorescence positive for some species (Microsporum).

2. Laboratory Confirmation

KOH Preparation (10-20% KOH Mount): Scrape scales from active border, dissolve in KOH on slide, examine microscopically for septate branching hyphae. Simple, quick, definitive. Available at all dermatology clinics. Sensitivity 50-80%.

Fungal Culture (Sabouraud's Dextrose Agar): Identifies species and enables antifungal sensitivity testing. Takes 2-4 weeks. Important for resistant/recurrent cases. Available at medical college mycology labs.

Antifungal Sensitivity Testing (AFST): For Terbinafine-resistant cases. MIC determination by broth microdilution. Increasingly necessary in India's resistance epidemic. Reference lab test.

3. Dermoscopy

Dermoscopic Examination: Shows comma hairs, corkscrew hairs, broken hairs in Tinea Capitis. Identifies active border and subtle satellite lesions in Tinea Corporis.

4. Diagnostic Criteria

Clinical diagnosis: Annular scaly plaque with active border + central clearing. Confirmed: Clinical features + positive KOH mount showing hyphae. Resistant cases: KOH positive + culture with species ID + AFST to guide therapy.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. Topical Antifungals (Mild/Limited Disease — <3 lesions, <3cm)

Luliconazole Cream 1%: Currently most effective topical for Indian dermatophytosis. Apply once daily for 2-3 weeks. Effective against Terbinafine-resistant strains. IADVL recommended first-line topical.

Sertaconazole Cream 2%: Alternative effective topical. Apply twice daily for 4 weeks. Broad-spectrum anti-dermatophyte.

Amorolfine Cream 0.25%: Once daily application for 2-3 weeks. Good efficacy against T. mentagrophytes.

DO NOT USE: Steroid-antifungal combination creams (Clobetasol/Betamethasone + Ketoconazole combinations) — these WORSEN infection, cause steroid-modified tinea, and drive resistance.

2. Systemic Antifungals (Extensive/Recurrent/Resistant Disease)

Itraconazole: 200mg daily for 4-6 weeks (adults). Currently first-line systemic antifungal for Indian dermatophytosis (given Terbinafine resistance). Monitor LFTs. Pulse therapy: 200mg twice daily for 1 week/month for 3-4 months (for onychomycosis).

Terbinafine: 250mg daily for 2-4 weeks. Still effective for sensitive strains but resistance rates 30-50% in India. Check culture sensitivity before use if available.

Griseofulvin: 500-1000mg daily for 4-8 weeks. Older drug. First-line for Tinea Capitis in children (safe, effective). Available and affordable.

3. Adjunctive Measures

Antifungal Dusting Powder: Clotrimazole/Miconazole powder on body folds and feet after bathing. Keeps skin dry and prevents recurrence.

Treat All Family Members Simultaneously: Screen and treat all household contacts. Simultaneous treatment prevents ping-pong reinfection.

diseases.ayurvedic_medicines:
Karanja / Pongamia (Pongamia pinnata): Classical Kushthahara and Krimighna. Karanja oil (Karanja taila) has published antifungal activity. Apply on lesions twice daily. Traditional remedy for Dadru (ringworm in Ayurveda).

Neem (Azadirachta indica): Broad-spectrum antimicrobial. Neem oil or neem paste applied topically. Neem water bath. Published antifungal activity against dermatophytes.

Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Topical turmeric paste (haldi + neem paste) applied on ringworm lesions. Curcumin has published anti-dermatophyte activity. Traditional first-aid remedy across India.

Arka Kshara (Calotropis procera): Milky latex applied on ringworm lesions in traditional practice. Strong antifungal properties. Use cautiously — can irritate skin.

Chakramarda / Cassia (Cassia tora / Senna tora): Classical Dadru-hara herb. Leaf paste applied on ringworm. Published antifungal activity. Seeds also used.

Vidanga (Embelia ribes): Krimighna herb with antifungal properties. Vidangadi Lepa (paste) for external application.

Mahamanjishthadi Kwatha: Internal blood purifier for chronic skin conditions. 15-30ml twice daily.

Gandhaka Rasayana (Sulfur preparation): Classical for Kushtha/Dadru. Purified sulfur with antifungal/antibacterial properties. 250-500mg twice daily with milk. Practitioner supervision.
diseases.india_specific:
Epidemic Proportions in India: India is experiencing an unprecedented epidemic of chronic, recurrent, treatment-resistant dermatophytosis since 2017-2018. Trichophyton mentagrophytes/indotineae (new species) is the predominant pathogen — resistant to Terbinafine. An estimated 50-60 million Indians affected.

Steroid-Modified Tinea (Tinea Incognito): The BIGGEST India-specific problem — widespread over-the-counter use of potent topical corticosteroid-antifungal combinations (Clobetasol + Ketoconazole + Gentamicin creams sold as 'ring guard') temporarily suppresses symptoms but spreads infection, creates atypical morphology, and drives resistance. IADVL has campaigned against these 'steroid combination creams'.

Hot, Humid Climate: India's tropical climate (heat + humidity + sweating) creates ideal conditions for dermatophyte growth. Tight synthetic clothing, shared towels, and overcrowded living worsen transmission.

Antifungal Resistance — Terbinafine: Terbinafine resistance rates in India now exceed 30-50% for T. mentagrophytes/indotineae — unprecedented globally. This has made standard treatment protocols inadequate for India.

Family/Community Transmission: Ringworm spreads rapidly within families and communities via shared clothing, towels, beds, and direct skin contact. Entire families often need simultaneous treatment.
diseases.important_notes:
CRITICAL — STOP Using Steroid Combination Creams: The widespread OTC use of Clobetasol+Ketoconazole creams (sold as 'ring guard' type products) is the PRIMARY reason India's ringworm epidemic is worsening. These creams temporarily reduce itch/redness but SPREAD the fungus, create bizarre atypical lesions, and drive antifungal resistance. IADVL/Indian Dermatology strongly condemns their use.

Treat the Entire Family: Ringworm spreads within households. If one family member has it, screen and treat everyone simultaneously. Shared towels, clothes, and bed linen should be washed in hot water.

Complete Full Course of Antifungal: Stopping treatment when itching reduces but infection is not fully cleared → recurrence and resistance. Complete full 2-6 week course even after visible improvement.

Ayurvedic Topicals Can Help: Neem oil, Karanja oil, and turmeric paste have genuine antifungal properties and are safe, affordable first-line topical options for mild ringworm. They can be used alongside or as initial therapy for limited lesions.

Keep Skin Dry & Wear Cotton: Dermatophytes thrive in moisture. Wear loose cotton clothing, change underwear daily, use antifungal powder in body folds, dry thoroughly after bathing. Avoid synthetic tight clothing especially in Indian summer.
confirmation_tests_curated: Clinical Recognition: Dermatophytosis is diagnosed CLINICALLY in most cases by the characteristic annular lesion with active scaly border and central clearing. Wood's lamp (UV) fluorescence positive for some species (Microsporum).

KOH Preparation (10-20% KOH Mount): Scrape scales from active border, dissolve in KOH on slide, examine microscopically for septate branching hyphae. Simple, quick, definitive. Available at all dermatology clinics. Sensitivity 50-80%.

Fungal Culture (Sabouraud's Dextrose Agar): Identifies species and enables antifungal sensitivity testing. Takes 2-4 weeks. Important for resistant/recurrent cases. Available at medical college mycology labs.

diseases.name: Scabies
diseases.tests:
1. Clinical Diagnosis (Primary Method)

Clinical Recognition: Scabies is diagnosed CLINICALLY: Intense nocturnal pruritus + characteristic distribution of papulovesicular rash + burrows in web spaces/wrists + family members/contacts also itching. Lab confirmation rarely needed.

2. Microscopic Confirmation

Skin Scraping with Mineral Oil / KOH: Scrape burrow with scalpel blade (#15), place on slide with mineral oil or 10% KOH, examine under microscope. Positive if mite, eggs, or scybala (fecal pellets) seen. Sensitivity only 40-50% — negative scraping does NOT rule out scabies.

Dermoscopy (Handheld Dermatoscope): Shows 'jet with contrail' sign — triangular dark structure (mite head) with trailing burrow. Quick, non-invasive, increasingly available. Sensitivity 86-97%.

3. Additional Tests

Ink Burrow Test: Apply ink to suspected burrow area, wipe with alcohol. Ink tracks along burrow, making it visible. Simple bedside test.

Serum IgE: Elevated in chronic scabies (allergic response). Non-specific.

4. Diagnostic Criteria

Scabies diagnosed by: (a) Typical itch + distribution pattern + contact history (clinical diagnosis sufficient), OR (b) Demonstration of mite/eggs/scybala on scraping (definitive). In endemic community settings, itching + papular rash in characteristic distribution = treat empirically.
diseases.generic_medicines:
1. Topical Scabicidal Treatment (First-Line)

Permethrin 5% Cream: Apply from neck down to entire body surface, leave for 8-14 hours (overnight), wash off. Repeat after 1 week. Safest and most effective topical. Current first-line per WHO/IADVL. Safe in children >2 months and pregnant women.

Benzyl Benzoate 25% Lotion: Apply neck-down, leave overnight, wash off. Repeat for 2-3 consecutive nights. Effective but causes stinging/burning. Avoid in children <2 years. Widely available and affordable in India.

Sulfur Ointment 5-10%: Apply for 3 consecutive nights. Oldest scabicide. Safe in pregnancy and infants. Messy, smelly but effective. Often combined with petroleum jelly base.

2. Oral Treatment

Ivermectin: 200μg/kg single oral dose. Repeat after 1-2 weeks. Convenient, systemic. First-line for crusted/Norwegian scabies. Used in community MDA. AVOID in children <15kg and pregnant women.

3. Treatment of Contacts & Fomites

Treat ALL Household Contacts Simultaneously: Even asymptomatic contacts (incubation period 4-6 weeks). This is the MOST IMPORTANT step to prevent reinfection.

Wash Bedding & Clothing: Hot water wash (>60°C) or seal in plastic bag for 72 hours (mites die without human host in 48-72 hours). Change bed linen on treatment day.

4. Secondary Infection Management

Mupirocin Ointment 2%: For impetigo/secondary bacterial infection. Apply twice daily for 5 days.

Oral Antibiotics (Cephalexin/Amoxicillin-Clav): For extensive secondary infection.

5. Itch Management (Post-Treatment)

Cetirizine/Hydroxyzine: Post-scabicidal itch may persist for 2-4 weeks (dead mite allergic reaction). Antihistamines for symptom relief. Reassure patient that persistent itch ≠ treatment failure.
diseases.janaushadhi_medicines:

Ivermectin Tablets 12mg: Oral scabicide. Strip of 1.

Cetirizine Tablets IP 10mg: For itch relief. Pack of 10.

Mupirocin Ointment 2%: For secondary infection.

Approximate Jan Aushadhi Prices (MRP incl. taxes):
